authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. construction safety regulation requiring employers to maintain safety programs, inspect job sites, materials, and equipment through competent persons, and block unsafe equipment use.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety, equipment condition, and release gating
used_for: boundary between coordination and safety authority

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.250 - General requirements for storage
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary OSHA construction standard for aisles, passageways, ramps, grading, and storage conditions that materially affect equipment delivery, staging, and site-readiness decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Obtain a Certificate of Occupancy
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official city building-department guidance defining legal occupancy, final approval, temporary occupancy, and required approvals before completion or occupancy claims can be made.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Permit, final approval, and turnover gating
used_for: refusal of completion claims without final authority

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Mechanical Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official building-department inspection guidance identifying permit-and-plan availability, operational tests, and equipment protection conditions before final inspection scheduling.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Manual Section 3-5 Control of Work
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public-owner construction operations manual covering order of work, subcontracting, coordination with other entities, permits, submittals, records, and equipment controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Kahua Quick Reference Guide: Project Closeout - Delegated Projects
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal public-owner closeout workflow guide specifying closeout document upload, substantial completion milestone entry, and notification steps before a project can be marked complete.

authoritative_sources.title: Contractor requirements, certifications, and qualifications for fire alarm and water-based fire suppression
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal facilities maintenance standard defining hazardous-condition reporting, work-order scope control, permits and licenses, coordination with building managers, inspection reports, and maintenance records for equipment-focused field service.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Field-service work-order coordination and hazardous-condition escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Contractor's Guide to Contract Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal construction-administration guide covering schedules, submittal registers, shop drawings, equipment data, daily reports, O&M manuals, installed property lists, and closeout conferences.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Submittal, daily-report, and turnover evidence control

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm site, jurisdiction, work-order, equipment class, delivery state, trade, and owner context before any coordination recommendation is made.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Delivery, staging, and site-readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect permit status, approved-plan access, laydown constraints, access conditions, and safety observations needed to determine whether equipment can be delivered or staged.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Submittal, schedule, and owner coordination
workflow_stages.description: Validate work orders, submittal registers, equipment-data packages, delivery dates, startup sequence, and assigned owners before packaging the handoff.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Inspection, startup, and authority boundary enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Require operational-test evidence, inspection readiness, licensed trade boundaries, and hazardous-condition escalation without overriding formal authority.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closeout, turnover, and closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Require O&M manuals, as-builts, installed property records, milestone completion, and destination-owner acceptance before confirming transfer or completion.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: Work orders, service tickets, and installation packages
Permit status records, approved plans, inspection requests, and correction notices
Delivery receipts, laydown plans, inventory updates, and installed-property records
Equipment data submittals, shop drawings, manufacturer instructions, and startup checklists
Daily reports, site logs, safety observations, and hazardous-condition notes
Change orders, punch lists, O&M manuals, warranties, as-builts, and closeout packages
systems_of_record: Schedule management tools and milestone logs
Tenant authority matrices and policy libraries

allowed: Assess whether available site, permit, inspection, delivery, staging, startup, and closeout evidence supports a safe handoff, block, or escalation posture.
Produce evidence-backed handoff summaries, dependency trackers, routing confirmations, and closure notes.
Request missing prerequisites and source-of-truth records before continuing.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, hazardous conditions, or site constraints materially change the answer.
Licensed trade, engineering, design, code, or owner approval authority is needed.
Tenant policy and public guidance conflict, or owner ambiguity remains unresolved.
refuse: Permit approval, code interpretation, or regulator-facing commitments
Licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record judgment, energization approval, or startup release authority
Closure, turnover, or completion confirmation without sufficient evidence

domain_failure_modes: Treating a generic delivery or service update as sufficient while ignoring actual permit, inspection, site-readiness, or safety prerequisites.
Routing equipment handoffs without required submittals, equipment data, startup evidence, or installed-property records.
Collapsing coordination into code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, or shutdown or startup approval authority.
Closing work without O&M manuals, as-builts, correction resolution, or destination-owner acceptance evidence.
Ignoring schedule, owner, or system-of-record conflicts that materially affect who should receive the next handoff.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority

unresolved_ambiguities.note: Public sources show that startup, final inspection, temporary occupancy, and turnover order can vary materially by locality and owner contract. The pack therefore requires tenant and jurisdiction retrieval before recommending completion or release posture.
Public baseline sources do not resolve every equipment class. The pack explicitly refuses to infer trade, engineering, or manufacturer-authorization boundaries when the tenant has not supplied them.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack delivery because all material variances are converted into explicit tenant-fact or authority-boundary requirements.
Research output constrains the lane to coordination, handoff packaging, blocker management, and closure confirmation rather than execution or approval authority.
